2606.31349.title: PGUDA: Pressure-Guided Unsupervised Domain Adaptation with Cross-Modal Knowledge Distillation for sEMG-Based Gesture Recognition
2606.31349.paper_summary: Surface electromyography (sEMG)-based gesture recognition has emerged as a promising technology for natural human-computer interaction. However, its practical deployment remains challenging due to severe performance degradation caused by feature distribution discrepancies across different subjects and recording sessions. Although domain adaptation (DA) techniques are commonly employed to mitigate such discrepancies, conventional methods often struggle to effectively aligning sEMG features, primarily due to their inherent stochasticity and the scarcity of labeled data. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel Pressure-Guided Unsupervised Domain Adaptation (PGUDA) framework, which leverages the robustness and stability of pressure signals to introduce a cross-modal knowledge distillation strategy that transfers consistent physical semantics across modalities. Specifically, a teacher network trained on pressure signals guides an sEMG student network on unlabeled target domains, thereby regularizing the representation learning process with transferable and modality-invariant knowledge. Extensive experiments conducted on a self-collected multimodal dataset involving eleven subjects validate the effectiveness of the proposed PGUDA framework. The results demonstrate that our proposed PGUDA achieves leading performance in both cross-subject and cross-session classification tasks, achieving average accuracies of 58.08% and substantially outperforming existing DA approaches. Notably, PGUDA exhibits remarkable label efficiency: it attains classification accuracy comparable to fully supervised benchmarks while requiring only 5% of labeled data for teacher network training. This framework offers a robust and data-efficient solution that can significantly reduce the calibration burden in practical sEMG-based gesture recognition systems.
2606.31349.paper_summary_zh: 表面肌電圖 (sEMG) 基於手勢識別已成為自然人機互動的一項有前景的技術。<lb/>然而，由於不同受試者和錄製會話之間的特徵分佈差異所造成的性能嚴重下降，其實際部署仍然面臨挑戰。<lb/>儘管通常採用領域適應 (DA) 技術來減輕這些差異，但傳統方法往往難以有效對齊 sEMG 特徵，主要是由於其固有的隨機性和標記數據的稀缺性。<lb/>為了解決這些限制，本文提出了一種新穎的壓力引導無監督領域適應 (PGUDA) 框架，該框架利用壓力信號的穩健性和穩定性，引入了一種跨模態知識蒸餾策略，將一致的物理語義轉移到不同模態之間。<lb/>具體而言，對壓力信號進行訓練的教師網絡指導在未標記目標領域上的 sEMG 學生網絡，從而用可轉移且模態不變的知識正則化表示學習過程。<lb/>在涉及十一名受試者的自收集多模態數據集上進行的廣泛實驗驗證了所提出的 PGUDA 框架的有效性。<lb/>結果表明，我們提出的 PGUDA 在跨受試者和跨會話分類任務中達到了領先的性能，平均準確率為 58.08%，並顯著超越了現有的 DA 方法。<lb/>值得注意的是，PGUDA 展現出卓越的標籤效率：它在僅需 5% 的標記數據進行教師網絡訓練的情況下，達到了與完全監督基準相當的分類準確率。<lb/>這一框架提供了一種穩健且數據高效的解決方案，能夠顯著減少實際基於 sEMG 的手勢識別系統中的校準負擔。
2606.31349.author: Yurui Liu et.al.
2606.31349.authors: Yurui Liu, Xiao-Cong Zhong, Qisong Wang, Xuefu Wang, Dan Liu, Jinwei Sun
